2003-08-01.title: Paris Journal; Back to Barricades: Liberty, Equality, Sisterhood
2003-08-01.abstract: French National Assembly mounts exhibit titled 'Mariannes of Today,' with photos of 13 women, eight of them North African Muslim and African immigrants, who have been chosen to reflect country's ideal face, or faces; since French Revolution, 'Marianne' has been idealized embodiment of freedom, supposedly immortal figure that gets new look occasionally; current display is part of government's campaign to make citizens of foreign origin, particularly Muslims, feel French; goal of other groups is to address problems of underclass women of tough, immigrant suburbs; photos (M)

2003-08-01.title: 2 G.I.'s Killed and 5 Wounded After Lull in Iraq Attacks
2003-08-01.abstract: Explosion on road near Baghdad airport and shooting attack 50 miles northeast of capital kills two American soldiers and wounds five, ending rare two-day stretch of relative quiet in which no soldiers died; American administrator L Paul Bremer 3d reiterates that national elections could be held in Iraq as soon as one year from now; his military counterpart Lt Gen Ricardo S Sanchez says 'foreign fighters' have traveled to Iraq to fight American troops; suspects they are linked to Al Qaeda; takes pains to distance himself from recent claim by Deputy State Sec Richard Armitage and other American officials that American troops are closing in on Saddam Hussein; in Jordan, two of Hussein's three daughters arrive with their children after King Abdullah issues order admitting them on humanitarian grounds; their husbands were executed by their father in 1996; photo (M)

2003-08-01.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-08-01.abstract: RUSSIA: Moscow Prosecutors Ousted; THE HAGUE: Life Sentence For Bosnian Serb; ITALY: Berlusconi Ally Returns; FRANCE: Riviera Fires Ease;.

2003-08-01.title: New Law Raises Obstacles to Israeli-Palestinian Marriages
2003-08-01.abstract: Israeli Parliament votes to block Palestinians who marry Israelis from becoming Israeli citizens or residents, erecting new legal barrier as Israel finishes first section of new physical barrier against West Bank Palestinians; supporters of bill call it necessary bulwark against infiltration by terrorists, as well as way to preserve Israel's Jewish majority; opponents call it racist measure that threatens to divide thousands of families or force them out of Israel; Israel's 1.2 million Arab citizens are far more likely than Israeli Jews to marry Palestinians; Israel solicits construction bids to build 22 new homes in settlement in Gaza Strip, even though new peace plan calls on Israel to freeze settlement construction; photo (M)

2003-08-01.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: Berlusconi Ally Returns
2003-08-01.abstract: Claudio Scajola, former Italian Cabinet member and ally of Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi, returns to Cabinet year after his inappropriate remarks forced his resignation (S)

2003-08-02.title: New Tape, Said to Be From Hussein, Urges Iraqis to Resist
2003-08-02.abstract: Middle East television stations broadcast new audiotape, apparently recorded by Saddam Hussein, in which almost desperately sounding voice urges Iraqis not to cooperate with American occupiers; US forces are attacked west of Baghdad; photo (S)

2003-08-02.title: Truck Bombing at Russian Military Hospital Kills 35; Officials Blame Chechen Separatists
2003-08-02.abstract: Truck loaded with explosives blows up next to military hospital in Mozdok in southern Russia, killing at least 35 people and wounding dozens more; map; authorities blame Chechen separatists; bombing is latest in series of suicide attacks (M)

2003-08-02.title: G.O.P. Senator Criticizes Bush On Liberia Case
2003-08-02.abstract: Sen John Warner, Republican chairman of Armed Services Committee, criticizes Bush administration for preparing plans to send US troops to Liberia without making convincing case to Congress, which is in recess; comments on Senate floor after Pentagon abruptly cancels detailed briefing; charges administration is not adhering to doctrine of spelling out risks and national interest involved; remarks by usually staunch Bush ally come as two of three Navy ships carrying 2,300 marines are set to arrive off Liberian coast (M)

2003-08-02.title: Ancient Art at Met Raises Old Ethical Questions
2003-08-02.abstract: Metropolitan Museum's sumptuous exhibit of Mesopotamian antiquities includes Naram-Sin limestone fragment that was unknown until it surfaced in home of New York collector four years ago, illustrating ambiguous, often murky, world in which such objects are traded; debate becomes more pointed with recent looting in Iraq; photos of three objects in Met show, only one of which has clear provenance; museum director Philippe de Montebello says that even objects without clear records may come from legitimate sources, such as longstanding private collections; experts say including such objects abets illicit trade that destroys archaeological sites; unclear history of Naram-Sin fragment, involving collector Jonathan P Rosen, archaeologist Donald P Hansen and Met curator Joan Aruz, explained (M)

2003-08-02.title: World Briefing | Africa: Rwanda: 105 Convicted In Ethnic Killings
2003-08-02.abstract: Rwandan court convicts 105 people for roles in mass killings of 1994 (S)

2003-08-03.title: Taylor Ready to Leave Office Aug. 11, but With a Condition
2003-08-03.abstract: Charles G Taylor says he will step down as president of Liberia in nine days, but refuses to save whether he will leave country, condition for any American military involvement in peacekeeping there; spokesman says question can be addressed only after lifting of war crimes indictment; West African officials say first of 1,500 Nigerian troops will still arrive on Aug 4; government forces begin their heaviest counterattack in days on rebels attacking Monrovia; photos (M)

2003-08-03.title: Americans Killed Near Baghdad
2003-08-03.abstract: Two American soldiers and their interpreter are killed when homemade bomb explodes under their convoy on outskirts of Baghdad; grenade attack on convoy near Shumayt kills one US soldier and wounds three (S)

2003-08-03.title: U.S. and Japan at Odds Over G.I. Prosecutions
2003-08-03.abstract: US and Japan fail to resolve differences on how to treat American military personnel suspected of serious crimes in Japan (M)

2003-08-03.title: Israelis, and Visitors, Revel in a Summer of Calm
2003-08-03.abstract: Israelis enjoy three-month suspension of violence declared by Palestinian groups, but doubt if respite will last; peace plan is trying to reacquaint both Israelis and Palestinians about benefits of tranquillity to generate popular support for political concessions needed to sustain it; each day without terrorist attack is good for tourism; photos (M)

2003-08-03.title: Arafat Orders Militants Jailed, but They Balk
2003-08-03.abstract: Yasir Arafat acts to put some 20 Palestinian militants under detention at his compound at Ramallah on West Bank, but detainees refuse to be sent to jail in Jericho, as demanded by Israel as condition for withdrawal of its troops from Ramallah; standoff reflects division between Arafat and Prime Min Mahmoud Abbas over how to handle negotiations with Israel (M)

2003-08-04.title: With Iraqi Courts Gone, Young Clerics Judge
2003-08-04.abstract: Religious courts run by upstart young clerics spring up in Najaf and Baghdad following disappearance of Saddam Hussein's government, with dockets covering all types of criminal and civil cases that normal courts would hear if they were functioning; Islamic court's decisions, which include permission to kill, could have dubious legality in regular court system, assuming it is restored; many aggrieved Iraqis, feeling they have no other place they can trust for legal rulings, are flocking to these courts; courts have no enforcement power and are not recognized by either American occupation forces or Iraq's other Muslim religious authorities; grand ayatollahs either deliberately ignore courts or say their decisons lack religious significance because no local senior cleric advises them; officials of regular court system, still trying to recover under American tutelage, expect religious courts inevitably to fade; photos (M)

2003-08-04.title: Palestinians Allow Militants to Stay in Arafat's Compound
2003-08-04.abstract: Palestinian officials back away from attempt to arrest Palestinian militants holed up in Yasir Arafat's West Bank compound; decision eases stalemate with militants, who refused to cooperate, but it prompts Israel to accuse governing Palestinian Authority of buckling to terrorists; most militants are members of Al Aksa Martyrs Brigades, violent offshoot of Arafat's mainstraem Fatah faction; Palestinian officials are urging Israel to pull its forces out of Ramallah and other West Bank cities, in compliance with international peace plan; Israel says Palestinian Authority must first comply with its own obligation under plan to begin collecting illegal weapons and take other steps against militants; photo (M)

2003-08-04.title: The Leo-conservatives
2003-08-04.abstract: For the past few weeks, US President George W. Bush has been surrounded by a secretive circle of advisors and public relations experts, giving rise to all kinds of conspiracy theories and debates. It's been said that the group's idol is German Jewish philosopher Leo Strauss.

2003-08-04.title: Indicted Hyundai Executive Plunges to Death in Seoul
2003-08-04.abstract: Chung Mong Hun, South Korean industrialist who was business driver behind South's policy of reconciliation toward North Korea, dies in fall from headquarters of his family company, Hyundai Asan, an apparent suicide; he faced trial on charges that he secretly passed $100 milion in money from South Korean government to North Korea in 2000, as way to ensure that Kim Jong Il would receive Kim Dae Jung, then president of South Korea; Chung made numerous trips to North to promote business projects with North; returned from his latest trip to North 10 days ago to learn that government prosecutors, operating independently of Pres Roh Moo Hyun, had placed travel ban on him; Chung, through his aides, said money sent to North Korea was payment for business dealings; photo (M)

2003-08-04.title: Food and Peace Just a Memory In Liberian City
2003-08-04.abstract: Foraging for food is too treacherous even for most daring Liberian civilians, as fighting between Pres Charles Taylor's loyalists and rebels seeking to overthrow him shower Monrovia with bullets and mortar shells; in country that can easily grow enough rice to feed itself, food supply is dwindling to dire levels, especially in government-held parts of capital; malnutrition in children is climbing; hospitals scramble to feed patients; civilians in Monrovia plug up their windows with cushions and pray for protection from stray bullets; capital has been pounded by rebel group for more than two weeks; it is demanding Taylor's ouster; he has promised to step aside, but has remained barricaded in his Executive Mansion; United Nations, United States government and West African heads of state have promised to help end bloodshed, but peacekeepers have yet to arrive; photos (M)

2003-08-05.title: Deadly Car Bombing Shakes Marriott Hotel in Jakarta
2003-08-05.abstract: The explosion seriously damaged the lower floors of the 33-floor hotel, killed at least 10 people and injured nearly 150.

2003-08-05.title: Killed in Iraq
2003-08-05.abstract: Defense Department releases names of three Americans killed in Iraq (S)

2003-08-05.title: U.S. May Reduce Aid Plan to Get Israel to Halt Barrier
2003-08-05.abstract: Bush administration is considering reducing loan guarantees for Israel that were approved by Congress this spring in effort to press Israel to halt construction of barrier separating its citizens from Palestinians; any such punitive step would mark change in Pres Bush's longstanding efforts to avoid any kind of confrontation with government of Prime Min Ariel Sharon; Bush administration officials say they are being told by Palestinian leaders that barrier has cut Palestinians off from farms, homes, schools and workplaces; say Palestinians view barrier as de facto border for Israel in West Bank and Gaza Strip, even though future boundary of Israel and Palestinian state is supposed to be negotiated in talks between two sides; religious conservatives in Republican Party, including Rep Tom DeLay, suggest that Bush should not press Israel to adopt peace plan calling for Palestinian state (M)

2003-08-05.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: Herr Berlusconi
2003-08-05.abstract: Italian Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi, in interview with German newspaper Bild, says he has German work ethic; only last month Berlusconi said German member of European Parliament resembled concentration camp guard (S)

2003-08-05.title: U.S. Pressure on Israel
2003-08-05.abstract: The Bush administration is considering a cut in $9 billion in loan guarantees for Israel as a way to press for a halt in construction of a barrier separating Israelis from Palestinians, administration officials said.    Article, Page A3.

2003-08-06.title: U.S. Civilian Killed in Blast Near Tikrit
2003-08-06.abstract: American civilian working in Iraq for Halliburton subsidiary KBR is killed when he drives mail truck over homemade bomb north of Tikrit; US forces carry out 25 raids and detain 141 people in hunt for Saddam Hussein (S)

2003-08-06.title: World Briefing: Africa
2003-08-06.abstract: KENYA: Suspects Tied To Hotel Bombing; ZAMBIA: More Charges For Ex-Leader;.

2003-08-06.title: Arrests in London Killing Linked to Child Trafficking
2003-08-06.abstract: Recent breakthrough in British investigation of murder of Nigerian child found headless and limbless in Thames River in 2001 brings new scrutiny to growth of child trafficking; riot police arrest 21 people in raids linked to investigation of child's death in apparent ritual sacrifice; children smuggled from Africa have turned up in several British cities (M)

2003-08-06.title: Blair Aide Apologizes for Comment About Dead Arms Scientist
2003-08-06.abstract: Tom Kelly, spokesman for Prime Min Tony Blair, issues unusual public apology for likening late weapons scientist David Kelly to Walter Mitty, fictional character who leads humdrum life but dreams of heroic deeds; latest dispute stemming from accuracy of intelligence data erupts day before funeral of Kelly, who apparently committed suicide after testifying at inquiry about reasons for going to war in Iraq (M),

2003-08-06.title: U.S. Expected to Resume Patrols Over Colombia for Drug Flights
2003-08-06.abstract: Pres Bush is expected to approve resumption of anti-drug surveillance flights in Colombia, including forcible grounding or destruction of planes suspected of smuggling narcotics (S)

2003-08-07.title: World Briefing | Asia: South Korea: Life Term For Subway Arsonist
2003-08-07.abstract: Arsonist Kim Dae Han, who set fire to South Korean subway train last February in Taegu killing 198 people and injuring 147, is sentenced to life in prison (S)

2003-08-07.title: World Briefing | Europe: Iceland: Whale Hunts To Resume
2003-08-07.abstract: Iceland plans to reinstitute whaling for scientific research; government policy sets goal of killing 38 whales by end of next month; International Whaling Commission, which has passed resolution against research whaling, sees practice as cover for commercial gain (S)

2003-08-07.title: Pentagon Asserts Baghdad Bombing Won't Alter U.S. Tactics
2003-08-07.abstract: The bombing of the Jordanian embassy in Baghdad will not disrupt American military operations in Iraq, a high-ranking Pentagon official said today.

2003-08-07.title: At Least 11 Die in Car Bombing at Jordan's Embassy in Baghdad
2003-08-07.abstract: The attack, which also wounded more than 60, was the most violent of several incidents in Baghdad in the past 24 hours.

2003-08-07.title: Man Receives Death Sentence for Bali Blasts That Killed 202
2003-08-07.abstract: An accused member of the Islamic militant group Jemaah Islamiyah is the first defendant to be convicted in the Bali case.

2003-08-08.title: U.S. Panel on Religious Freedom Drops China Visit, Protesting Curbs
2003-08-08.abstract: American federal agency, Commission on International Religious Freedom, calls off its visit to China to examine religious freedom after Chinese authorities impose what group calls 'unacceptable last-minute conditions' on trip, including pressure not to visit Hong Kong; visit had been subject of diplomatic negotiations for six months (M)

2003-08-08.title: World Briefing | Asia: China: Antiterror Exercises
2003-08-08.abstract: Shanghai Cooperation Organization, made up of China, Russia, Tajikistan, Kyrgyzstan, Kazakhstan, and Uzbekistan, hold antiterrorism drill involving more than 1,000 troops (S)

2003-08-08.title: F.B.I. to Lead Inquiry Into Embassy Bomb Attack in Iraq
2003-08-08.abstract: The involvement of the F.B.I. appeared to underline the concerns of American officials that the attack represents the work of organized terrorists.

2003-08-08.title: Archbishop of Canterbury Acts to Prevent Rift on Gay Bishop
2003-08-08.abstract: The spiritual leader of the world's 79 million Anglican Christians will convene a meeting of Church leaders to try to avert a formal schism over the confirmation of a gay bishop.

2003-08-08.title: World Briefing | Asia: North Korea: Chinese Envoy Arrives
2003-08-08.abstract: Chinese Vice Foreign Minister Wang Yi visits North Korea to exchange views in advance of next month's six-party meeting scheduled in Beijing; China is seeking to renegotiate mutual defense treaty (S)

2003-08-09.title: SOUTH AFRICA SAYS IT WILL FIGHT AIDS WITH A DRUG PLAN
2003-08-09.abstract: South African government, bowing to regional and international clamor for more vigorous attack on AIDS epidemic, says it will develop plan to offer drugs to combat HIV virus to infected people through its public health system by Oct 1; announces shift in drug policy after special meeting to assess financial costs of national drug plan and to explore treatment options; about 5 million South Africans, over 11 percent of population, are infected with HIV, including 20 percent of population aged 15 to 49; epidemic poses major threat to nation's future by primarily affecting young sexually active adults and incapacitating tradition extended family system that cares for sick and orphaned relatives; Pres Thabo Mbeki and his top aides have long resisted national drug programs; government is also expected to provide prevention programs; Bush administration lauds plan (M)

2003-08-09.title: A New European Keeps a Wary Eye on America
2003-08-09.abstract: Interview with Romano Prodi, who is set to begin final year of his five-year term as European Commission president; he stresses importance of European unity; expresses concern that United States understands neither value of united Europe nor difficulty in securing unity; photo (M)

2003-08-09.title: Mideast Calm Is Disrupted By Exchanges on 2 Fronts
2003-08-09.abstract: Three Palestinians, including two suspected gunmen from Islamic militant group Hamas, and Israeli soldier are killed in firefight and ensuing protest during what Israeli Army calls hunt for bomb makers in Nablus, West Bank; Hamas leaders threaten retaliation, but say they remain committed to cease-fire; incidents apparently do not reflect policy switch by either side; Hezbollah guerrillas from Lebanon shell Israeli military posts in occupied areas of Golan Heights, firing across Israel's northern border for first time since Jan; Israel responds using helicopter gunships and artillery; map; photo (M)

2003-08-09.title: Italy Slides Into Recession
2003-08-09.abstract: Government reports Italy slid into recession for first time in 11 years, with gross domestic product falling 0.1 percent, Apr-June, same decline as in first quarter (S)

2003-08-09.title: Iraqi Trailers Said To Make Hydrogen, Not Biological Arms
2003-08-09.abstract: Government officials say Defense Intelligence Agency experts believe that most likely use for two mysterious trailers found in Iraq was to produce hydrogen for weather balloons used in artillery practice, not biological weapons; agency and Central Intelligence Agency officials say agencies still stand by their May 28 report that trailers were for making biological arms; it was not previously known that majority of Defense Intelligence Agency's engineering teams had come to disagree with that report; officials say teams had not completed work at time report was drafted and that their views were not taken into account at that time (M)

2003-08-10.title: Displaced Chinese Ancestors Reburied in U.S.
2003-08-10.abstract: Thousands of deceased Chinese are being exhumed by their descendants and reburied in America, often in places they have never visited in life; descendants fear destruction of their ancestors' graves as cemeteries in China are bulldozed to make way for farms, offices and housing tracts; posthumous emigration reverses tradition that dates to gold rush, when Chinese-American would dig up laborers who had perished in California and ship them back to their ancestral villages; photo (M)

2003-08-10.title: Rebels Move Fighting Into Burundi's Capital
2003-08-10.abstract: Hutu rebels in Burundi, who for last few years have limited their fighting to rugged rural areas of country, are making bold incursions into heart of capital, Bujumbura; city's predominantly Tutsi residents are suddenly forced to confront fierce ethnic divisions that they thought were resolved by peace accord; photos; map (M)

2003-08-10.title: Tough New Point System Tames Some Wild Italian Drivers
2003-08-10.abstract: Italian drivers start obeying traffic signals and buckling seat belts in response to new point system that provides for revocation of license after certain number of offenses (M)

2003-08-10.title: Hezbollah Shelling Kills Teenager; Israel Strikes Back
2003-08-10.abstract: Israel described its response to the attack as a limited mission against Hezbollah artillery positions.

2003-08-10.title: Paraguay Mennonites Find Success a Mixed Blessing
2003-08-10.abstract: German-language culture and religious traditions that Mennonites brought to Chaco region of Paraguay are threatened by influx of Spanish speakers who are drawn from other parts of Paraguay by healthy farming economy Mennonites have created; Mennonites, who first arrived in 1927, now make up less than half of population of Filadelfia in heart of Chaco; photo of Orlando Penner, Paraguay's first Mennonite senator, who says Mennonites should integrate more with other Paraguayans (M)

2003-08-11.title: Yokosuka Journal; Ripples From Perry's Ships Are Still Felt in Japan
2003-08-11.abstract: Port city of Yokusuka, Japan, commemorates 150th anniversary of arrival of Commodore Matthew C Perry, who forced Japan to open up to international trade and relations; this led to collapse of regime that had ruled feudal Japan in isolation and peace for more than two centuries, and then to modern Japan's scramble to catch up with West and grab Asian empire; historians say Perry has sunk into obscurity in United States, but he is most widely known foreign historic figure in Japan; photo of re-enactment of his landing (M)

2003-08-11.title: British Inquiry Opens Into the Suicide of Weapons Expert
2003-08-11.abstract: Intelligence officers registered concern at the way the prime minister's government represented the threat from Iraq's weapons.

2003-08-11.title: On Knife Edge, Liberia Awaits Taylor's Move
2003-08-11.abstract: Pres Charles G Taylor of Liberia, cornered by fierce, well-armed insurgency, promises to give up his office on Aug 11 and blames United States for his ouster; calls rebels 'surrogate force' of US in internationally televised farewell address; Liberians wonder how soon he will leave country, as rebel enemies have demanded, and whether rebels will cede control of Free Port of Monrovia, strategic prize of unsurpassed value that could either be country's gateway to fresh bloodshed or its portal to peace; Taylor has been offered asylum in Nigeria; White House has made his departure precondition for any direct American engagement in bringing peace to country after 14 years of on-and-off civil war; photos; map (M)

2003-08-11.title: NATO Takes Over Peacekeeping Force in Afghanistan
2003-08-11.abstract: In its first mission beyond Europe's frontiers, NATO took formal control of Afghanistan's peacekeeping force today.

2003-08-11.title: Glen to Glen, No Smoking At the Pub
2003-08-11.abstract: Antitobacco movement is gaining ground in Western Europe; Ireland is expected to become first European country to ban smoking in pubs on Jan 1; move is so bold and contentious in country devoted to pub culture that few in Europe, and even fewer in Ireland, believe it will succeed; Norway and Netherlands have approved prohibitions on smoking in bars and restaurants that will take effect in future; France is close to approving law that would stiffen penalties on selling cigarettes to minors; question remains how vigorously new laws will be enforced; many European countries already have antismoking laws that are widely ignored; photo (M)

2003-08-12.title: World Briefing | Asia: Taiwan: Suspect Chemical Taken From North Korean Freighter
2003-08-12.abstract: Japan's Kyodo News reports Taiwan authorities have seized 40 tons of phosphoros pentasulfide, chemical that can be used to manufacture rocket fuel, from North Korean freighter (S)

2003-08-12.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Second 9/11 Trial
2003-08-12.abstract: Moroccan-born Abdelghani Mzoudi goes on trial in Hamburg (Germany) for his part in Sept 11, 2001 terrorist attacks in New York and Washington; friend of Mohamed Atta, he is charged with 3,066 counts of abetting murder and terrorism; Moroccan Mounir el-Motassadeq, also part of Hamburg cell, is currently serving 15-year sentence for aiding in attacks (S)

2003-08-12.title: Relic of War Adds to Strain In Beijing Ties With Tokyo
2003-08-12.abstract: At least 36 people in Qiqihar, China, have taken ill after unearthing mustard gas Chinese officials say was left behind by Japanese troops at end of World War II, straining Japanese-Chinese ties; visit by Japanese Foreign Ministry team to probe accident overshadows separate Japanese delegation sent to mark 25th anniversary of normalization of ties; bitter history is also complicating China's choice of Japanese or European technology for high-speed rail link between Shanghai and Beijing; tens of thousands of Chinese have signed Web-based petitions calling on government not to award $12 billion contract to Japan; leaders from both countries publicly try to play down divisions; map; photo (M)

2003-08-12.title: 2 Suicide Bombings, One Claimed by Hamas, Kill 2 Israelis
2003-08-12.abstract: The attacks today inflicted the most serious blow yet to the American-led plan to find peace between Palestinians and Israelis.

2003-08-12.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-08-12.abstract: NORTH KOREA: Talks Likely This Month; INDIA: 27 Feared Dead In Copter Crash; HONG KONG: Police Buy Riot Gear; TAIWAN: Suspect Chemical Taken From North Korean Freighter;.

2003-08-13.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-08-13.abstract: HOT, HOTTER, HOTTEST; BELGIUM: People Smugglers Sentenced;.

2003-08-13.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-08-13.abstract: SOUTH KOREA: U.S. Officer Held After Body Found; SOUTH KOREA: U.S. Military Plane Crash Kills 2; NEPAL: Talks With Rebels To Resume; INDONESIA: 15-Year Term Sought For Cleric; CHINA: Mines Closed After Blast;.

2003-08-13.title: World Briefing | Europe: Hot, Hotter, Hottest
2003-08-13.abstract: Record temperatures continue across Europe with Swiss canton Grisons reaching 106.7 degrees, hottest day in Switzerland's history; at least 100 heat related deaths in France, and 45 more in other parts of Europe, have been reported (S)

2003-08-13.title: Proposal for Kosovo Autonomy Offered Instead of Independence
2003-08-13.abstract: Serbia rejects calls for independence of United Nations-administered Kosovo province; adopts draft document proposing substantial autonomy, within federation of Serbia and Montenegro; says progress toward building multiethnic society in province has been unsatisfactory and marked with ethnic discrimination; Prime Min Zoran Zivkovic says non-Albanians, especially Serbs, face greater security risks there than ethnic-Albanians; Serbia calls for indictment of ethnic Albanians suspected of war crimes (M)

2003-08-13.title: 2 ISRAELIS KILLED IN SUICIDE ATTACKS BY ARAB BOMBERS
2003-08-13.abstract: Two Palestinian teenagers blow themselves up in separate suicide bombings, killing two Israelis and inflicting new blow to fragile American-led plan to forge peace between Palestinians and Israeli; attacks jar Israelis after over month of relative calm; Al Aksa Martyrs Brigade claims responsibility for first attack, Rosh Haayin; Hamas claims responsibility for second, near entrance to Ariel, Jewish West Bank settlement; Prime Min Ariel Sharon again calls for Palestinian Authority to crack down on militants; has yet to launch military reprisal; White House spokeswoman echoes Sharon's call to disarm militants; State Sec Colin L Powell pledges attacks will not derail peace efforts; Hamas leaders deny its attack marks end to self-imposed cease-fire; call it retaliation for killing of two Hamas members in Israeli raid in Nablus; Palestinian Authority condemns attacks; blames Israel for violating terms of peace plan; photo (M)

2003-08-14.title: World Briefing: Americas
2003-08-14.abstract: ARGENTINA: Legislators Back Amnesty Repeal; CHILE: General Strike;.

2003-08-14.title: Lady Diana Mosley, Fascist Who Dazzled, Is Dead at 93
2003-08-14.abstract: Lady Diana Mosley, who embraced fascism and admired Hitler, dies at age 93; was one of England's famous Mitford sisters; photo with her late husband, Sir Oswald Mosely, leader of British Union of Fascists (M)

2003-08-14.title: Officials Say Shortages Could Cause More Riots in Basra
2003-08-14.abstract: British troops remain on edge in wake of riots that have shaken Basra, Iraq, for two days; 21 British soldiers are either stabbed or wounded by thrown bricks or rocks during riots; more unrest is feared if fuel supplies and electric service does not improve quickly; American soldier is killed and two others wounded by homemade bomb north of Baghdad; another soldier is killed and one wounded when their vehicle strikes homemade bomb south of Tikrit; photo; maps (M)

2003-08-14.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-08-14.abstract: GERMANY: Heat Wave Exposes Old Bombs; RUSSIA: North Korea Security Pact Proposed;.

2003-08-14.title: China Allows Sale of Animal Tied to SARS
2003-08-14.abstract: Chinese agriculture officials in Beijing are allowing farms to resume selling masked palm civets, species related to mongoose that may have been source of SARS virus; Chinese provinces retain discretion to bar human consumption of animals (S)

2003-08-15.title: World Briefing | Americas: Argentina: Leftists Arrested In 'Dirty War' Inquiry
2003-08-15.abstract: Two former Montonero guerrilla leaders are arrested for ordering comrades to return from exile between 1976 and 1983 even though they knew they were likely to be captured, tortured and killed; Montonero leader Mario Firmenich is in Spain and may face extradition for his crimes (S)

2003-08-15.title: Toronto Calmly Copes but Fears the Night
2003-08-15.abstract: There was an edge of apprehension that the blackout would last into the night on Thursday, but during the day, the mood in Toronto was calm and cooperative.

2003-08-15.title: U.S. Apologizes for Baghdad Mosque Incident
2003-08-15.abstract: United States military apologizes for incident that deeply angered Iraqi religious leaders, when soldiers in helicopter forced down flag near mosque in overwhelmingly Shiite district of Baghdad; flag episode outraged residents of Sadr City, poor but fervent Shiite neighborhood; large protest followed, leading to death of one Iraqi and wounding of four others by American troops (M)

2003-08-15.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-08-15.abstract: GREECE: Quake Hits Islands; RUSSIA: Plan To Join Islamic Body; RUSSIA: Rights Office Robbed; GERMANY: Ex-Marine To Be Extradited To Britain; LONDON: Security Report Criticizes Police; BRITAIN: No Love, Dears;.

2003-08-15.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: No Love, Dears
2003-08-15.abstract: Bristol (England) government receptionists and security have been directed to eliminate use of dear or love in addressing public; they are instead advised by City Council leader Barbara Janke to use sir or madam; change has raised some controversy (S)

2003-08-16.title: Names of the Dead
2003-08-16.abstract: Defense Dept confirms deaths of three American service members in Iraq (S)

2003-08-16.title: Idi Amin, a Brutal Dictator of Uganda, Dies at 80
2003-08-16.abstract: Idi Amin's eight year reign of terror in Uganda encompassed widescale killing, torture and dispossession of multitudes and left the country pauperized.

2003-08-16.title: Liberians Cross Capital's Former Front Line as Aid Arrives
2003-08-16.abstract: Tens of Thousands of Liberians burst through barricade that separated warring government and rebel forces in Monrovia after hearing that rebels have withdrawn from area; Monrovians who had been trapped by shelling and gunfire walk freely through city for first time in weeks; all are survivors of series of brutal battles in last three months, culmination of 14 years of fighting led in large part by Pres Charles G Taylor, who has stepped down; many Monrovians applaud arrival of United States marines, whose presence changed political climate overnight; first ship to arrive for more than month carries first relief shipment to Liberia since June; photo (M)

2003-08-16.title: World Briefing | Africa: Morocco: Separatists To Release Prisoners
2003-08-16.abstract: Polisario Front, Western Sahara rebel group fighting 30-year war for independence, announces release of 243 Moroccan prisoners after renewed request by Spanish President Jose Maria Aznar; United Nations Security Council has shown support for referendum on independent Western Sahara (S)

2003-08-16.title: Herb Offers Hope to Africans Ravaged by Malaria
2003-08-16.abstract: Malaria epidemic in Burundi from late 2000 to summer of 2001 generated 3 million cases in country of 6.5 million people; about 85 percent of people live in areas at risk of the disease, which preys on those who have not yet built up natural defenses, particularly children; rate of resistance to common malarial drugs is so high in Burundi that they are deemed ineffectual; increasingly, health experts are recommending combination treatment, called artemisinin, that comes from Chinese herb; it has been endorsed by World Health Orgn and United Nations Children's Fund and has been issued by Doctors Without Borders; treatment does nothing to stop spread of disease; first line of defense is keeping mosquito off human skin, no easy task in Africa; photo (M)

2003-08-17.title: Professor Teaches Change in His Indian Village
2003-08-17.abstract: Prof Jagadish Shukla, Indian-American climatologist in Maryland, returns each year to home village of Mirda, in impoverished Uttar Pradesh, where he has used money earney in America to pay for relatives' education, marriages, homes and, in lean times, food; photos; Shukla is also setting aside ten percent of his income for more ambitious projects, such as community college and medical dispensary, that are bringing change to place where clock is still set by sun (M)

2003-08-17.title: Talks Stall on the Security Transfer of 2 West Bank Cities
2003-08-17.abstract: Israeli and Palestinian commanders failed to reach an agreement Sunday night over the planned transfer of two West Bank cities to Palestinian control.

2003-08-17.title: A China Odyssey Sheds Light on a Murky Past, and Hints at the Future
2003-08-17.abstract: David W Chen article on return to father's hometown of Chen Dong in Fujian Province, China, for ceremony honoring his grandparents; photo; map; looks back on twenty generations of Chens who are chronicled in local genealogy, and describes lives of relatives today (M)

2003-08-17.title: As North Korean Men Turn to Business, Women Join the Army
2003-08-17.abstract: Women are increasingly filling baggy brown uniforms of North Korea's vaunted army, including many drafted for all-female units; analysts including Dr Alexandre Y Mansourov cite flight of population to China, famine and fact that many young men are ducking seven-year draft to try to make money in small businesses (M)

2003-08-17.title: Iraqi Athletes Scour Field of Nightmares
2003-08-17.abstract: Iraqi athletes who bear physical or mental scars inflicted by Uday Hussein when he controlling Olympic Committee are helping rebuild athletic programs; Ahmed al-Samarrai, former coach of national basketball team who fled to Switzerland in 1983, now heads interim sports committee set up by US occupation to clear out old clubs and replace them with democratically elected groups; photo; Samarrai recalls torture and humiliation of training system that began in primary school (M)

2003-08-18.title: A Shiite Burial Ground Awaits Foreign Faithful
2003-08-18.abstract: Valley of Peace Cemetery in Najaf, Iraq, sacred burial ground for Shiite Muslim world, is receiving trickle of bodies smuggled into Iraq after years in which bodies were denied entry by Saddam Hussein's government; cemetery workers expect something of a windfall once borders are reopened for bodies, convinced that tens of thousands Shiites buried elsewhere will be dug up and shipped to Najaf; photos (M)

2003-08-18.title: Talks Stall on the Security Transfer of 2 West Bank Cities
2003-08-18.abstract: Israeli and Palestinian negotiators fail to reach agreement on Israel's planned transfer to Palestinian security control of West Bank cities of Qalqilya and Jericho, even though both sides show some new flexibility to try to avert feared outbreak of violence; stumble in talks comes after Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat criticizes planned withdrawal as 'wasting time'; photo (M)

2003-08-18.title: Tomsk Journal; His Joy Is to Lampoon The Business of Russia
2003-08-18.abstract: Profile of Tomsk, Siberia, entrepreneur Yuri M Lirmak, who delights in lampooning Russia's transition from socialism to free enterprise and views capitalism with touch of cynicism; Tomsk is busy getting fixed up for its 400th anniversary next year; map; photo (M)

2003-08-18.title: Israeli-Arab Talks Over West Bank Stall
2003-08-18.abstract: Israeli and Palestinian commanders failed to reach an agreement over Israel's planned transfer of control over two West Bank towns to the Palestinians, even though both sides had shown some new flexibility in an effort to thwart a feared outbreak of violence.     Negotiators canceled talks for today and said they would meet again tomorrow, and an Israeli security official said the talks were being conducted with ''good spirits.''

2003-08-18.title: Attacks in Iraq Might Be Signs Of New Tactics
2003-08-18.abstract: Series of attacks raises new concerns that Iraqi insurgents who have been targeting American soldiers may be widening their strikes to include civilian targets and economic sabotage; in turbulent 12-hour period, pipeline supplying much of Baghdad's water is blown up, huge fire is set off along oil pipeline in northern Iraq, and mortar attack on prison in Abu Ghraib leaves six Iraqis dead and scores wounded; Reuters cameraman Mazen Dana is killed by American fire while filming outside prison; officials say motives for attack on prison are unknown but suggest that shelling, like the sabotage of pipelines, may be part of wider effort to destabilize Iraq and drive out Americans; officials estimate that it will take two weeks to repair pipeline and that its loss will cost Iraq $7 million per day; in southern Iraq, Danish soldier and two Iraqis are killed in firefight, first military casualty for the Danish; photo (M)

2003-08-19.title: World Briefing | Europe: Spain: Effects Of Oil Spill Will Last 10 Years
2003-08-19.abstract: Barrie de la Maza Foundation, studying effects on Galician coastline, Spain, of oil spill last November from tanker Prestige, reports it will take 10 years and $3 billion for environmental recovery (S)

2003-08-19.title: Blair's Points Led to Rewrite Of Dossier, Panel Is Told
2003-08-19.abstract: Judge conducting inquiry into background of disputed British intelligence dossier on Iraq's unconventional weapons is told that document underwent 'substantial rewrite' to address points raised by Prime Min Tony Blair in weeks before its publication last Sept; communication is made public at start of second week of judicial hearings into apparent suicide of weapons expert David Kelly; it has focused attention on public suspicions that Blair's office may have manipulated intelligence to exaggerate threat posed by Iraq's arms; stated focus of inquiry is death of Kelly and whether public pressure drove him to take his life, but hearings are amounting to wider examination of government's credibility and conduct and standards of BBC (M)

2003-08-19.title: Army Says Car Bomb Is Cause; Number of Injured is Unknown
2003-08-19.abstract: A car bomb ripped through a hotel used by the U.N. today, damaging the building and rocking the surrounding neighborhood.

2003-08-19.title: Bush Blames Attack on 'Enemies of the Civilized World'
2003-08-19.abstract: President Bush telephoned Secretary General Kofi Annan of the United Nations to extend his condolences and offer assistance.

2003-08-19.title: Liberian House and Senate Left in Tatters. Is Peace Next?
2003-08-19.abstract: It will likely take a lot more than the power-sharing agreement signed Monday to rebuild Liberia's government.

2003-08-20.title: Italian Puzzle: The Land That Doesn't Seem to Fit
2003-08-20.abstract: Italy's awkward place among Western nations is symbolized by Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi, who has garnered more attention than respect in recent months; past Italian leaders have failed to make a mark on world scene because of their parochialism, but Berlusconi, who wants to change that, illustrates another of Italy's enduring problems: porous, oddly fuzzy boundaries between public and private domain; photo (M)

2003-08-20.title: Heat Death Toll Forces a Shocked France to Question Itself
2003-08-20.abstract: The staggering number of deaths in France is finally drawing the nation's attention to who died and how.

2003-08-20.title: Military Explosives Used in Baghdad Blast, F.B.I. Says
2003-08-20.abstract: A Soviet-built 500-pound bomb, mortar rounds and grenades were used to blow up part of a U.N. compound, the F.B.I. said.

2003-08-20.title: Questions About Role of World Agencies in Hot Spots
2003-08-20.abstract: Grief at devastating bombing of United Nations compound in Baghdad cascades through organization, and several officials and diplomats begin to raise troubling questions about incident and how organization's 'moral shield' could have become so porous; officials continue to express determination to continue humanitarian and political outreach of Sergio Vieira de Mello, one of at least 17 civilian UN workers to perish in blast; excluding peacekeepers, about 240 civilian UN workers in trouble spots around the world have died in line of duty since 1992 (M)

2003-08-20.title: HUGE SUICIDE BLAST DEMOLISHES U.N. HEADQUARTERS IN BAGHDAD; TOP AID OFFICIALS AMONG 17 DEAD
2003-08-20.abstract: Suicide bomber drives cement mixer full of explosives into side of United Nations compound in Baghdad, Iraq, blowing it up, killing 17 people and wounding at least 100; compound was filled with hundreds of people responsible for array of relief and reconstruction duties; among dead are top UN officials, including Sergio Vieira de Mello, secretary general's special representative in Iraq, whose office was just above site of impact; bombing is apparently calculated to destroy any sense of security for people charged with reviving Iraq in aftermath of war; also seems intended to intensify guerrilla war under way against American soldiers and to increase domestic pressure on Pres Bush, who faces growing unhappiness about course of American occupation; speculation about who carried out attack ranges across wide spectrum of possibilities, but immediate focus of attention is on Ansar al-Islam, militant Islamic group suspected of plotting attacks against Western targets in Baghdad; photos; map; drawings (L)

2003-08-21.title: Venezuelan Opposition Files to Seek Referendum on Chávez
2003-08-21.abstract: Venezuelan opposition groups present election officials with 3.2 million signatures calling for removal of Pres Hugo Chavez, first step in new effort to oust him through constitutional recall referendum; hundreds of thousands of antigovernment demonstrators clog streets of Caracas in support of recall effort; National Electoral Council has 30 days to verify validity of signatures, and referendum must be held within 60 days after that; Bush administration has strongly supported referendum to resolve political tumult in country; major obstacles still face opposition, which is being led by haphazard coalition of big business, labor groups and news organization owners; photo (M)

2003-08-21.title: Tokyo Journal; At Long Last, the Salarymen Are Given Their Due
2003-08-21.abstract: Millions of Japanese watch television series Project X: Challengers, which documents technological and other innovations of common Japanese salarymen; each advance recalls age when values went unquestioned; in Japan, country groping its way out of long economic malaise, program illuminates recent past when all seemed possible, inspiring feelings of validation and nostalgia among older Japanese, envy and desire among some younger ones; photos (M)

2003-08-21.title: Weapons Expert Talked of Being 'Found Dead,' Witness Says
2003-08-21.abstract: The judge investigating the death of the David Kelly was told today that he talked of being "found dead in the woods."

2003-08-21.title: Israeli Military Raises Pressure After Bombing
2003-08-21.abstract: Israeli military reinforces positions around Ramallah and three other West Bank cities as Israel ratchets up pressure on Palestinian leadership to strike against military groups after suicide bomber kills 20 people on bus in Jerusalem; Palestinian Prime Min Mahmoud Abbas meets with leadership of Palestine Liberation Orgn, including Yasir Arafat, to seek its support for possible action against militants; meeting produces condemnation of bombing but no specific plan of action; Prime Min Ariel Sharon convenes his top security advisers to weigh possible Israeli military action in reprisal for bombing; one senior official says Israel will take action because Palestinian leadership is 'doing nothing'; Israel appears to be seeking to calibrate its response, to show it will back up its threats of force without signaling it is abandoning Abbas or Pres Bush's peace plan; photo (M)

2003-08-21.title: Former Iraqi Official Known as 'Chemical Ali' Is Captured
2003-08-21.abstract: Ali Hassan al-Majid, the cousin and trusted adviser of Saddam Hussein who earned a reputation for ruthlessness, is in U.S. custody.

2003-08-22.title: A U.S. Soldier in Afghanistan Dies of Injuries
2003-08-22.abstract: American military command says American Special Operations soldier died of injuries he received during military operations in eastern Afghanistan; soldier died during what appears to be offensive by Taliban fighters in Paktia Province, which lies on border with Pakistan; other incidents in Paktia Province have been reported recently (M)

2003-08-22.title: Sydney Journal; Where Wombats Roved, and Aborigines Sketched
2003-08-22.abstract: Paul S C Tacon, leading Australian anthropologist, describes unusually rare and pristine cache of ancient Aboriginal rock art found in cave in rugged wilderness not far from Sydney; says more than 200 images in giant mural stretch from 4,000 years ago to late 18th century when white settlers first ventured onto Australian soil; cave was discovered by hikers, but Tacon is only expert to have seen it so far; photos (M)

2003-08-22.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Court Backs Head Scarves
2003-08-22.abstract: Germany's highest court rules that Muslim woman cannot be fired for wearing Islamic head scarf at work (S)

2003-08-22.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: New Tests On Colas
2003-08-22.abstract: Indian Minister of Health reports government tests on local soft drinks, including Pepsi and Coca-Cola, show that most have only slight increase in pesticide levels over European Union standards; earlier testing by independent environmental agency reported much higher levels and caused drop in soft drink sales; Coca-Cola India president Sanjiv Gupta expresses company satisfation with new test results; photo (S)

2003-08-22.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-08-22.abstract: RUSSIA: Helicopter Not Found; GERMANY: Court Backs Head Scarves; SERBIA: 44 Charged In Killing Of Premier; GREECE: Impatience With Young Tourists;.

2003-08-23.title: U.S. Expects Informal Direct Talks With North Korea
2003-08-23.abstract: Bush administration officials, preparing for new round of talks with North Korea, says Bush will not offer North any specific economic or diplomatic rewards to abandon its nuclear arms program but will not block offers by other nations; says Asst Sec James A Kelly will probably have informal one-on-one conversations with his North Korean counterpart during Beijing talks (M)

2003-08-23.title: The Illusions Of Progress
2003-08-23.abstract: Analysis: Bombing of Israeli bus by Palestinian suicide bomber and return of Israeli troops to their old positions in West Bank and Gaza erase images of peace both sides sought to project, leaving new Palestinian leadership of Prime Min Mahmoud Abbas as last and quickly fading illusion of progress; Bush administration now faces grim reality that his peace plan has produced no basic change (M)

2003-08-23.title: World Briefing | Americas: Argentina: Iran Protests Ex-Envoy's Arrest
2003-08-23.abstract: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Hamidreza Assefi condemns Great Britain's arrest and possible extradition to Argentina of former Iranian diplomat Hadi Soleimanpour; Soleimanpour is accused of involvement in 1994 bombing of Jewish community center in Buenos Aires that killed 85 people (S)

2003-08-23.title: BUSH ORDERS MOVE TO FREEZE ASSETS OF HAMAS CHARITIES
2003-08-23.abstract: Pres Bush orders Treasury to block and freeze assets of militant Palestinian group Hamas's six top leaders and five charities based outside United States that administration says help finance Hamas; holds Hamas's claim of responsibility for bus bombing in Israel that killed 20 people shows group is terrorist organization committed to undermining peace process; move, which will probably affect small amount of assets in US, is politically important as first by US against Hamas's political wing, which provides social services and relief aid; is victory for Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon and other officials who have long argued that charitable groups tied to Hamas are conduit for financing terrorism; risks fueling anti-US anger among Palestinians who see Hamas as integral part of their lives; four of five charities are based in Europe, where leaders have thus far made careful distinction between groups' military and political wings; US scrambles to keep Bush's peace plan from collapsing in bombing's aftermath; chart of charities (M)

2003-08-23.title: Occupational choices : Tuscany lures expats with a passion for wine
2003-08-23.abstract: In Tuscany, Italy's premier wine-producing region, a number of non-Italians have begun to make a name for themselves as producers of quality wines especially in the Chianti area.

2003-08-24.title: Canada Links Arrest of 19 Pakistanis to Possible Terrorism Ties
2003-08-24.abstract: Canadian security officials say 19 students and other immigrants from Pakistan who were detained on Aug 14 used fraudulent documents to obtain and maintain immigrant status; says one was taking flying lessons at school near Pickering Nuclear Generating Station outside Toronto; court documents present picture of mysterious group of men who lived in apartments with only computers and mattresses and showed interest in explosives and in Pickering plant; government spokesman plays down threat to security (M)

2003-08-24.title: Berlusconi And Schröder: Gentlemen In Verona
2003-08-24.abstract: Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi and Chancellor Gerhard Schroder meet in Verona, putting aside summer of polemics and declaring relations between Italy and Germany to be 'excellent'; photo (M)

2003-08-24.title: As Serbia Slows Its Reforms, Europe Drifts Farther Away
2003-08-24.abstract: Slowdown of reform efforts in Serbia raises fears that country's entry into European Union will be delayed and that nationalist candidates will benefit; photo (M)

2003-08-24.title: French Winemakers Harvest a Hot Vintage
2003-08-24.abstract: French vineyards are harvesting their most promising crop in years, benefiting from blazingly hot temperatures that have been brutal to most everything else in Europe; hot summers and early harvests have normally meant great wines; photo (M)

2003-08-24.title: 3 British Soldiers Are Killed in Basra Attack
2003-08-24.abstract: Insurgents kill three British soldiers and wound one in Basra, Iraq; ethnic tensions lead to deaths of at least three Turkmen in Kirkuk; L Paul Bremer III, Iraq's top American civilian overseer, says it is problem that American forces did not have sort of intelligence that would have warned about Aug 19 bombing of United Nations headquarters in Baghdad; says any attempt to reimpose some fresh tyranny in Iraq will fail (M)

2003-08-25.title: 'Then we'll have another war'
2003-08-25.abstract: Republican Senator Richard Lugar talks about the failings of the Americans in Iraq and the road to reviving German-American relations.

2003-08-25.title: Iran Calls on Britain to Release Ex-Envoy Held in '94 Bomb Case
2003-08-25.abstract: Pres Mohammad Khatami of Iran demands apology from Britain for arrest of former diplomat accused of playing role in 1994 bombing of Jewish community center in Buenos Aires; also demands his release; former diplomat, Hadi Soleimanpour, was ambassador to Argentina during bombing and is first Iranian arrested in case; seven other Iranians were accused of involvement by Argentine court; bombing killed 85 people and wounded 300; Iran has repeatedly denied involvement in bombing; Soleimanpour was arrested in London, where court will decide whether he should be extradited to Argentina (M)

2003-08-25.title: Japan Detains the Sole Ferry to and From North Korea
2003-08-25.abstract: Citing safety violations, Japan detained today the lone ship that ferries passengers between Japan and North Korea.

2003-08-25.title: 3 Bombs in Russian City Kill at Least 3
2003-08-25.abstract: Three bombs exploded nearly simultaneously in a regional capital in southern Russia early today, killing at least 3 and wounding 17, some of them seriously, officials said.

2003-08-25.title: Real cure is based on plant that grows along China's rivers
2003-08-25.abstract: GUILIN, China:—  Artesunate may be considered the modern miracle cure for malaria, but the drug has the humble heritage of a traditional herbal extract used to treat fevers in China for more than 2,000 years.

2003-08-26.title: World Briefing | Asia: Afghanistan: Joint Operation Against Taliban
2003-08-26.abstract: Zabul, Afghanistan, Governor Hafizullah Khan reports joint operation between American ground troops and Afghan forces against suspected Taliban forces in southeastern Afghan mountains resulted in 14 anti-coalition casualties (S)

2003-08-26.title: Strains Emerge Between India and Pakistan After Bombings
2003-08-26.abstract: The death toll from two powerful bombs planted on Monday in taxis here rose to 52 today.

2003-08-26.title: World Briefing | Europe: Greece: Russian Mogul Stays In Custody
2003-08-26.abstract: Vladimir A Gusinsky, former Russian tycoon and political critic, remains in Greek custody pending possible extradition to Russia on charges of fraud and money laundering (S)

2003-08-26.title: Bombings Kill 3 in Russia
2003-08-26.abstract: Three bombs explode nearly simultaneously in regional capital of Krasnodar in southern Russia, killing at least 3 people and wounding 17 others; coordination of explosions prompts some officials to link them to wave of deadly acts stemming from war in Chechnya (S)

2003-08-26.title: Richard Hooper, 40, of the U.N., Expert in Politics of the Mideast
2003-08-26.abstract: Richard M Hooper, American official of United Nations who specialized in Middle East, dies in Aug 19 bombing on UN headquarters in Baghdad; he was 40 (S)

2003-08-27.title: Excavation's Finding at Mosque Site in India Could Fuel Dispute
2003-08-27.abstract: Archaeological Survey of India says it has found evidence structure resembling ancient Hindu temple may lie under 16th-century mosque, Ayodhya, whose destruction in 1992 by Hindu nationalist mob led to Hindu-Muslim clashes across India; Hindu nationalists say finding of court-ordered excavation proves temple should be built at site; vow protests if Uttar Pradesh state court rules against them; Muslim groups say remains are those of ancient mosque; map (M)

2003-08-27.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-08-27.abstract: INDIA: Chief Of Biggest State Quits; MYANMAR: New Prime Minister; VIETNAM: Sentence Cut;.

2003-08-27.title: Japan Frees North Korean Ferry After Holding It for Day in Port
2003-08-27.abstract: Japan releases Mangyongbong-92, ship that ferries passengers between Japan and North Korea, after detaining it for day in Niigata for alleged safety violations; many Japanese say widespread hostility toward North Korea was behind safety checks; North Korean calculation that Japan would not risk roiling relations in advance of six-country talks on North Korea's nuclear bomb program may have been behind ferry's first visit to Japan in seven months (M)

2003-08-27.title: Blair Aide Says Arms Expert Agreed With Britain on Iraq
2003-08-27.abstract: Defense Secretary Geoff Hoon said today that Dr. David Kelly told him he supported the government's position on war with Iraq.

2003-08-27.title: Postwar G.I. Deaths Now Exceed Toll From War
2003-08-27.abstract: Death toll of American troops killed in Iraq since Pres Bush declared end to major combat May 1 rises to 140, compared with 138 killed during war in Mar and Apr; most of soldiers who died during war were killed in combat; only 62 of deaths since May 1 were in combat situations, with 78 classified nonhostile (M)

2003-08-28.title: World Briefing | Europe: Ireland: Settlement In Abuse Case
2003-08-28.abstract: Kiltegan Fathers, Irish Roman Catholic order, pays $353,000 to man claiming he was sexually abused by Reverend Peter Kennedy in 1982; payout is largest clerical sex abuse settlement in Ireland to date (S)

2003-08-28.title: 2 U.S. Soldiers Are Killed and 5 Wounded in Iraq
2003-08-28.abstract: Two American soldiers are killed and five are wounded in separate attacks that used improvised explosive devices planted in and around Baghdad; in relatively peaceful Kurdish city of Sulaimaniya, two suspected Islamic militants and one Iraqi security official are killed in shootout; fears about security, especially around Baghdad, have caused several aid groups to reduce their presence or pull out of Iraq (M)

2003-08-28.title: 33 Are Killed in Stampede at a Hindu Festival
2003-08-28.abstract: Thirty-three people die and 74 others are injured in stampede at Hindu festival in Nasik, India, where tens of thousands of pilgrims try to bathe in holy waters of Godavari River in single day; government officials say crowds rushed forward too quickly after fence was opened to allow devotees to go for holy bath; stampedes have occurred at festivals in past, although last two passed without major incidents; Hindu nationalist politicians call for investigation into what went wrong; map (M)

2003-08-28.title: Unsolved Crimes Add to Plight of Serbs in Kosovo
2003-08-28.abstract: Unsolved murders of two youths in tiny Serbian enclave of Gorazdebac, in Albanian-dominated province of Kosovo, raise question about how long Kosovo can remain in its current limbo, uneasy United Nations protectorate still claimed by Serbia and still far short of independent Albanian-led state that majority of population wants; police investigating murders say local Albanians have done little to help reassure their Serbian neighbors; say gunmen were probably Albanians because they managed to vanish in Albanian neighborhood; killings came hour after Harri Holkeri, UN's new chief administrator in Kosovo, arrived to take up his post; this is third time since Nov that violent incident has occurred as international figure visits; photos; map (M)

2003-08-28.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Court Won't Extradite Turk
2003-08-28.abstract: German court rules Islamic militant Metin Kaplan, who is known as Caliph of Cologne, cannot be extradited to Turkey where he stands accused of terrorist plot against government; also rules he is no longer eligible for political asylum in Germany; photo (S)

2003-08-29.title: Munich Journal; Lindbergh the Family Man: Tales From Germany
2003-08-29.abstract: Three German siblings claim to be children of Charles A Lindbergh; Astrid Bouteuil and her brothers, David and Dyrk Hesshaimer, volunteer to take DNA tests; Bouteuil produces 150 letters that she says prove that Lindbergh conducted 17-year love affair with her mother Brigitte Hesshaimer, now deceased; Lindbergh family has said nothing publicly about claim but family lawyer has contacted Hesshaimers and plans to investigate matter; A Scott Berg, Lindbergh's biographer, professes to be stumped; says letters presented by Bouteuil seem genuine and notes Lindbergh's frequent travels and lengthy absences from his wife; photos; map (M)

2003-08-29.title: North Korea Says It May Test an A-Bomb
2003-08-29.abstract: North Korea's Deputy Foreign Minister Kim Yong Il tells diplomats from five other nations that his country plans to declare formally that it has become nuclear power and may test atomic bomb in near future; statement appears to sharply contradict spirit of six-party talks organized by China, and may buttress contention of hard-liners in Bush administration that reclusive North Korean government has no intention of reaching verifiable agreement to dismantle its nuclear program; China has prodded US, North Korea, South Korea, Russia and Japan to agree to second round of talks within two months (M)

2003-08-29.title: British Soldier Is Killed in Clash With a Mob in Southern Iraq
2003-08-29.abstract: British soldier is killed and another wounded when their convoy encounters angry mob of Iraqis blocking road in Ali as-Sharqi in southern Iraq as they return to base after arresting two suspected loyalists of Saddam Hussein; British troops arrest eight people in connection with shooting (M)

2003-08-29.title: World Briefing | Asia: Afghanistan: Heavy Fighting In The South
2003-08-29.abstract: Intense fighting continues in Zabul Province (Afghanistan) as American aircraft bombs suspected Taliban positions; there have been no American casualties, but at least 14 Afghans have died (S)

2003-08-29.title: France Calls for International Force in Iraq Under the U.N.
2003-08-29.abstract: French Foreign Min Dominique de Villepin calls for creation of international military force and provisional government in Iraq under United Nations authority; warns that unstable situation there could fall into 'anarchy'; says, in thinly veiled criticism of Bush administration's postwar strategy, that 'true change of approach is needed'; several nations have said they would consider sending troops to Iraq only if force is sanctioned by Security Council; Richard Perle, key adviser to Pentagon, says UN system is not adapted to deal with new threats, like international terrorism (M)

2003-08-30.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-08-30.abstract: AFGHANISTAN: G.I. Dies In Fall; PHILIPPINES: Defense Secretary Quits;.

2003-08-30.title: Killed in Iraq
2003-08-30.abstract: Defense Department confirms death of another American serviceman killed in Iraq

2003-08-30.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Inquest Into Diana's Death
2003-08-30.abstract: British officials announce impending formal inquiry into Paris car crash in which Diana, Princess of Wales, and her companion Emad Mohammed al-Fayed were killed; French inquiry into 1997 accident determined their chauffeur Henri Paul, who was also killed, was driving too fast (S)

2003-08-30.title: A Top Aide to Blair Resigns as a Dispute Over Iraq Rages
2003-08-30.abstract: Alastair Campbell resigns as Prime Min Tony Blair's director of communications and strategy as controversy rages in Britain over his role in portraying nature of Iraq's threat to West; dismisses notions that Blair's governing ability will be diminished by his departure; photo (M)

2003-08-30.title: China at Korea Talks: Taking Diplomacy Upstage
2003-08-30.abstract: China emerges as more assertive diplomatic power during six-nation talks in Beijing on North Korea's nuclear program; cajoled and badgered US, North Korea, South Korea, Russia and Japan to join negotiations and succeeds in pressing them to commit to another round despite obvious tensions between US and North Korea; assertiveness reflects alarm in top ranks of Communist Party that North Korean crisis could spiral out of control and perhaps also a new sense of engagement in world (M)

2003-08-31.title: For Two Battered Economies, Glimmers of Hope
2003-08-31.abstract: Some of sparkle may be back for Japan's economy, which has expanded for last six quarters, leading to forecasts of 2 percent growth rates for this year and next; company profits are up, business spending is up, bankruptcies are down, payrolls are expanding and Nikkei stock index has increased 36 percent since April; good economic news is scattering opposition to re-election of Prime Min Junichiro Koizumi at Sept 20 convention of governing Liberal Democratic Party; photo (M)

2003-08-31.title: German Stocks Are Rising, but Jobs, So Far, Are Not
2003-08-31.abstract: German economists and commentators dare to think that worst is over, even though economic growth for this year is expected to be only fraction above zero percent; say Germany is on path to recovery from one of its worst post-war periods of economic stagnation; graph shows DAX stock market index (M)

2003-08-31.title: Poor Nations Can Purchase Cheap Drugs Under Accord
2003-08-31.abstract: World Trade Orgn agrees to allow poor nations to import generic versions of expensive patented medicines, buying them from countries like India and Brazil without running afoul of trade laws protecting patent rights; breakthrough comes after US agrees to original proposal it rejected last December; several African delegates say agreement could save millions of lives (M)

2003-08-31.title: Now Free to Marry, Canada's Gays Say, 'Do I?'
2003-08-31.abstract: Skepticism about marriage is recurring refrain among Canadian gay couples, who have not rushed to marry in great numbers since June 10, when they became eligible; rather, extension of marriage rights has thrown gays in Canada into heated debate, akin to one that embroiled American civil rights movement in 1960's, over how much 'integration' is a good thing and what gay marriage should consist of; it is a debate that pits those who celebrate a separate and flamboyant way of life as part of counterculture against those who long for acceptance into mainstream; photos (L)

2003-08-31.title: Canada's Antiterror Laws Debated in Detention of 19 Suspects
2003-08-31.abstract: Canadian government lawyers argue for continued detention without charges of 19 Pakistani and Indian students and refugees arrested in Toronto who they suggest are connected to Al Qaeda and interested in attacking both nuclear power plant and CN Tower; Giuliano Zaccardelli of Royal Canadian Mounted Police says, however, that there is absolutely no evidence to suggest a terrorist threat anywhere in Canada related to investigation; apparent contradiction underscores muddled picture emerging from investigation of group (M)
